name: Serra de l'Ombria Loop
name_original: Las trialeras de la Vall d'Albaida — Circular desde el Paraje Pou Clar
description: This difficult 19.5-mile mountain biking trail in Vall d'Albaida offers a technical challenge and scenic views from Paraje Pou Clar.
region_intro: Mountain biking in Serra De L´Ombria offers diverse terrain within the Prebetic System in southern Valencia, Spain. The region is characterized by its limestone geology, featuring steep ravines on the eastern side and gentler slopes descending towards the Laguna de Salinas on the western side. Mediterranean ecosystems with Aleppo pines, holm oaks, and various shrubs cover much of the landscape, providing varied scenery for mountain bike trails. The highest point, Alt de les Pedreres, reaches 920 meters above sea level. Best mountain bike trails in Serra De L´Ombria The most popular mountain bike trail is Serra de l'Ombria Loop, a difficult 19.5 miles (31.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 50 minutes to complete, offering a comprehensive exploration of the mountain range. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Clariano River – Font Jordana loop from Ontinyent, a moderate 12.7 miles (20.4 km) path. This route follows the Clariano River and passes natural springs, showcasing the region's greener areas. Local mountain bikers also love the Montcabrer Viewpoint and Shelter – Cova de l'Habitació loop from Ontinyent, a difficult 29.0 miles (46.7 km) trail leading through varied landscapes and offering expansive views, often completed in about 4 hours 11 minutes. Mountain biking in Serra De L´Ombria is defined by limestone terrain, rugged ravines, and Mediterranean forests, with routes often combining forest tracks and some paved sections. The network offers options for a wide range of skill levels, from easy to highly challenging. The routes in Serra De L´Ombria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 3000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Serra De L´Ombria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Serra De L´Ombria?
A: Serra De L´Ombria offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 280 routes available on komoot. These range from easy paths suitable for beginners to highly challenging routes for experienced riders.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails in Serra De L´Ombria?
A: The terrain in Serra De L´Ombria is primarily limestone, featuring a striking contrast between steep, rugged ravines on the eastern side and gentler slopes descending towards the Laguna de Salinas on the western side. Routes often combine forest tracks with some paved sections, winding through Mediterranean ecosystems of Aleppo pines, holm oaks, and various shrubs.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Serra De L´Ombria?
A: Yes, Serra De L´Ombria has trails suitable for beginners. While many routes are moderate or difficult, there are 13 easy trails (rated S0-S1) that are perfect for those new to mountain biking or looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are the most challenging mountain bike routes in Serra De L´Ombria?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Serra De L´Ombria offers 151 difficult routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19431178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path to Agres – Alt del Forner de Verda loop from Ontinyent&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 51 kilometers with significant elevation gain, demanding advanced riding skills and good physical condition.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Serra De L´Ombria?
A: Yes, many routes in Serra De L´Ombria are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38289608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Clariano River – Font Jordana loop from Ontinyent&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 20.4 km circular path that follows the Clariano River and passes natural springs.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails in Serra De L´Ombria offer diverse natural and historical sights. You can encounter the expansive pools of crystal-clear water at Pou Clar, explore the varied landscapes within the Serra de l´Ombria-Pou Clar Municipal Natural Park, or discover historical elements like old mills and traditional snow cellars reclaimed by vegetation. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31450003" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pou Clar – Beautiful trail loop from Ontinyent&lt;/a&gt; is a great option to experience some of these features.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about Serra De L´Ombria?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 360 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, ranging from rugged ravines to gentler slopes, and the comprehensive exploration opportunities offered by routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e910838235" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de l'Ombria Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the highest point I can reach on a mountain bike in Serra De L´Ombria?
A: The highest point in the region is "Alt de les Pedreres" at 920 meters above sea level. Many routes will take you through varied elevations, offering panoramic views of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Are there any routes that offer good viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The region's varied topography provides numerous opportunities for expansive views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31572371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montcabrer Viewpoint and Shelter – Cova de l'Habitació loop from Ontinyent&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult trail specifically designed to lead through varied landscapes and offer impressive vistas.

Q: What kind of vegetation and wildlife can I expect to see?
A: Serra De L´Ombria is one of the greener areas in the municipality of Monòver, featuring typical Mediterranean vegetation such as Aleppo pines, holm oaks, kermes oaks, and arbutus trees. You'll also find a variety of shrubs like rosemary, thyme, and heather. Small mammals such as rabbits, squirrels, and weasels inhabit the area.

Q: Are there any historical sites accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, the region has historical elements that can be explored. These include the Serra's Castle and the Tower of Ria, both of Islamic origin, which were part of a historical defense system. The Serra de l´Ombria-Pou Clar Municipal Natural Park also contains old mills and traditional snow cellars.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain biking in Serra De L´Ombria?
A: Mountain biking in Serra De L´Ombria caters to a wide range of skill levels. While there are 13 easy routes, the majority are moderate (121 routes) or difficult (151 routes), meaning there are ample options for both intermediate and advanced riders seeking a challenge.
tips: Bocairent (Spanish: Bocairente) is a municipality in the Spanish province of Valencia. It is located in the Vall d’Albaida comarca. On the way there is a fantastic backdrop when you look at the houses built on the cliff. It looks like a scene from a medieval film. Top location!

A trialera that has it all, in sectors it is narrow but super entertaining if it is done descending on the way to the CV-655 road. While you circulate it you will have very nice views towards the entire Ontinyent valley

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of Las trialeras de la Vall d'Albaida mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: This is a difficult mountain bike ride, demanding good physical fitness and advanced riding skills. It's technically demanding, corresponding to approximately S3-S6 on the Single Track Scale (STS), meaning you should expect significant technical challenges, obstacles, and potentially sections where you'll need to dismount and push your bike.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The circular route is approximately 31.4 kilometers long and typically takes around 3 hours and 5 minutes to complete on a mountain bike.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends at the Paraje Pou Clar (Pou Clar natural pools). Parking is generally available in the vicinity of the natural park, but it's advisable to arrive early, especially during peak seasons, as specific parking regulations or capacity might apply.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is characterized by technical singletrack sections, often referred to as 'trialeras'. You should be prepared for roots, rocks, and steep inclines or descents. Some parts may require advanced bike handling skills.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Las trialeras de la Vall d'Albaida trail?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in natural areas, specific regulations for the Paratge Natural Municipal Serra de l'Ombria and the Pou Clar area may apply. It's best to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety on challenging terrain. Always check local signage for the most current rules.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee for this trail or the natural park?
faqs.answer: There are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required to ride this mountain bike trail or to access the Paratge Natural Municipal Serra de l'Ombria. However, access to the Pou Clar natural pools might have seasonal restrictions or capacity limits, especially during summer. Always respect local regulations and conservation efforts.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The Vall d'Albaida region generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking in spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable. Winter can be cooler but often still suitable for riding. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends at the beautiful Paraje Pou Clar, known for its crystal-clear waters. Along the way, you'll experience the natural beauty of the Vall d'Albaida region. The trail also passes near the historic town of Bocairent and includes sections of the challenging Magic Trail.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You will find sections that are part of the Camino del Alba, El Camí IT5TR4 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, Senda del Barranc dels Tarongers, and the Comunitat Valenciana. Fredes - el Pinós. It also briefly touches parts of the GR 7: Andorra - Strait of Gibraltar and the Ruta Màgica.

faqs.question: Is there water available on the trail or nearby?
faqs.answer: While the trail starts at the Paraje Pou Clar, which has natural water, it's crucial to carry sufficient water for the entire ride, especially given its length and difficulty. There are no guaranteed water refill points directly on the trail, so plan accordingly.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is explicitly rated as difficult and requires good physical fitness and advanced mountain biking skills. It is not recommended for beginners or families with young children. There are many technical sections that could be dangerous for inexperienced riders.
